title: banksia rufa subsp. tutanningensis
intro: Banksia rufa subsp. tutanningensis, also known as the Western Banksia, is a subspecies of shrub native to Western Australia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and is believed to offer health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Banksia rufa subsp. tutanningensis can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
